what_it_does: Maps the repository for a fresh agent by listing the intended read order, registry faces, proof and build scripts, application surfaces, mockup pieces, factory pack, documents, build timeline, situation facts, and working conventions.
observations: Opens with a REORG-1 note saying the repo was restructured after the map was written and that the read order still applies with docs prefixes.
Frames itself as a factual structural map rather than a quality assessment.
Instructs the reader to read THE-THINKING, LINEAGE, and HANDOVER before using this map.
Describes the repo as a Design DNA Pack made of tokens, registries, rules, proofs, surfaces, documents, and a distributable factory pack.
Lists 18 dna JSON registries with versions and roles.
Lists proof and build scripts, including DNA proofs, canon generation, surface CSS generation, and factory pack compilation.
Describes the gallery as the front door and the four-room house as Bench, Map, Almanac, and shell.
Documents server endpoints and local run commands.
Records that the repo contains untouched new source material for a future ingest.
States that the work was built across a long session with compactions and should be treated as needing rectification and unification before further work.
standouts: The file separates orientation, chronology, and structural navigation into THE-THINKING, LINEAGE, and this map.
The resolution circuit is stated explicitly as sequence to layout to organism to molecule to shape or connector to rule to token.
The map treats design assets, registries, runtime surfaces, proofs, documents, and git tags as one navigable system.
It includes operational cautions for future agents, including reporting honestly because Tim does not read or write the code.
conventions: Uses numbered sections with compact tables for registries, scripts, mockup groups, and timelines.
Uses bold file paths and short explanatory clauses for scan-friendly navigation.
Distinguishes factual mapping from judgment or drift assessment.
Marks generated files and live-served registries explicitly.
Uses uppercase emphasis for role labels such as START HERE, HOUSE, BENCH, MAP, and ALMANAC.
Keeps repo practices as lessons at the end rather than mixing them into the structural inventory.
concerns: The opening REORG-1 note says paths changed after the map was written, while much of the body still names older locations, so the file carries its own path-translation caveat.
It says the read order still holds with docs prefixes but also lists many paths without applying those prefixes inline.
It contains a large amount of operational and historical context in one onboarding file, which makes the boundary between map, runbook, and handover dense.
notes: Brief someone on this file as the repo's structural navigation ledger for agents, with read order, inventory, run commands, and known situation facts.
questions: Which listed paths should be read literally and which require the REORG-1 docs or archive prefix translation?
What specific rectification or unification work is intended after this onboarding map?
Are the counts and versions listed here still expected to be checked against disk before use?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file's actual content: it is a what-and-where map with read order, inventories, run instructions, and situation facts, while avoiding direct assessment of correctness or drift.
apparent_themes: design DNA as registries and generated surfaces
separation of identity language from engine
intention_signals: It seems intended to give a fresh agent enough structure to navigate the repo without mistaking the map for the deeper reasoning or for an assessment of current validity.
novelty: The file treats a design-language repository as a ledger of faces, proofs, surfaces, pieces, documents, tags, and agent operating lessons, with explicit read-order discipline before navigation.
contribution: Provides a file-local orientation layer that a fused system would keep as a navigation and operations ledger connecting registries, proof scripts, surfaces, pieces, factory output, documents, timeline, and agent conventions.
summary_for_embedding: ONBOARDING.md is a structural map of the counterpart-design repo, documenting read order, DNA registries, proofs, application surfaces, mockup pieces, factory pack, documents, build timeline, situation facts, and conventions for agents.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to orient a fresh agent to what exists, where it lives, how pieces connect, and what cautions apply before further work.
